page: Amazon Web Services m5ad.24xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by AMD EPYC 7571 processors built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 96 virtual CPUs across 48 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled, operating at a base clock speed of 2.2 GHz. The system is equipped with 384 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled as 3600 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store volumes, and network connectivity is supported by a 20 Gbps baseline bandwidth managed via the Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data reveals a clear performance tradeoff between single-core and multi-core workloads. Single-core CPU performance is categorized in the bottom 10% across stress-ng, Geekbench, and single-threaded compression tests. However, multi-core performance scales to average levels, as demonstrated by PassMark CPU scores and multi-threaded decompression. Memory bandwidth is a notable strength, ranking in the top 25% for both cached small-block and uncached large-block read and write operations. Cryptographic execution is also strong, aided by hardware-accelerated AES and SHA instruction sets.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the server leverages AMD EPYC processors and DDR4 memory to deliver high resource density and substantial local NVMe storage without the premium associated with high-frequency single-core architectures. This makes it a viable option for workloads that benefit from high memory bandwidth and local storage speed rather than raw single-threaded compute. Appropriate workloads include database operations, Redis deployments, static web serving, and multi-threaded batch processing. It is less suited for single-threaded tasks or large-scale LLM inference, where prompt processing for 70B models registers as weak.
description: Amazon Web Services m5ad.24xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 96 vCPUs on AMD EPYC 7571 processors, 384 GB of DDR4 RAM, and 3600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it provides a 20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks indicate a distinct tradeoff: single-core CPU execution is poor, ranking in the bottom 10%, whereas multi-core scaling and database operations perform at average levels. Notably, memory bandwidth is strong, placing in the top 25% for both cached and uncached operations. Cryptographic performance is also strong due to hardware-accelerated instructions. This hardware profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for resource-dense, memory-bound, and storage-heavy applications. It is best suited for database workloads, static web serving, and multi-threaded batch processing rather than single-threaded applications or large language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5ad.24xlarge is a general-purpose server with 96 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 384 GB DDR4 RAM, and 3600 GB local NVMe SSD storage, offering strong memory bandwidth and average multi-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5ad.24xlarge features 96 AMD vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 3600 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong memory bandwidth.
tagline: A resource-dense general-purpose platform combining AMD EPYC processors with high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage and strong memory bandwidth.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 384 GB DDR4 RAM powered by AMD EPYC 7571 processors
3600 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage for low-latency I/O
Strong memory bandwidth performance ranking in the top 25%
Average multi-core scaling and database operation benchmarks
Poor single-core performance representing a key architectural tradeoff
Suited for database operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded batch jobs
